title: Extraction of Total Nucleic Acid from Wastewater Using the Promega Wizard Enviro Kit
author: Tamara Walsky
materials: Promega Wizard Enviro Total Nucleic Acid Kit

steps.title: Dispense 40 mL of wastewater or...
steps.instruction: Dispense 40 mL of wastewater or pasteurized wastewater into a 50 mL conical screw-cap tube (Plastic consumables, Grenier Bio-One, 5622-7261, Any brand or size of plastic tube with a tight-fitting screw cap will work fine., https://www.usascientific.com/ ) Pasteurizing wastewater is optional. To pasteurize wastewater, incubate at 60 °C for 01:00:00 Please follow your institution’s biosafety guidelines.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Protease Solution
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Protease Solution. Mix well by inversion and incubate for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: 3000 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: 3000 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes . This step is designed to remove solids, to avoid clogging the PureYield™ Binding Column.

steps.title: Carefully decant 20 mL of the...
steps.instruction: Carefully decant 20 mL of the supernatant into each of two clean 50 mL conical screw-cap tube (Plastic consumables, Grenier Bio-One, 5622-7261, Any brand or size of plastic tube with a tight-fitting screw cap will work fine., https://www.usascientific.com/ ) Discard the 50ml conical tube containing the pellet into an appropriate biohazard waste container. Sometimes, the solids will produce a loose pellet. In this case, let the solids settle again before decanting or use a serological pipet to avoid transferring solids to the binding column. If you wish to process the pelleted solids to collect additional total nucleic acid, see Section 5.A. of the Wizard® Enviro Total Nucleic Acid Kit Technical Manual.

steps.title: To each tube containing 20 mL...
steps.instruction: To each tube containing 20 mL of the clarified supernatant, add 6 mL of Binding Buffer 1 (BBD) followed by 500 µL of Binding Buffer 2 (BBE).

steps.title: Mix well by inverting the tube...
steps.instruction: Mix well by inverting the tube gently 10 times, or until thoroughly mixed.

steps.title: Add 24 mL of isopropanol to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 24 mL of isopropanol to each tube.

steps.title: Mix well by inverting the tube...
steps.instruction: Mix well by inverting the tube gently 10 times, or until thoroughly mixed.

steps.title: Setup the vacuum manifold as follows (see Figure 1)
steps.instruction: Setup the vacuum manifold as follows (see Figure 1): remove vacuum port cap, attach a Reservoir Extension Funnel to the PureYield™ Binding Column, then connect the column to the vacuum manifold by pressing the nozzle gently into the vacuum port. Figure 1. The Reservoir Extension Funnel and PureYield™ Binding Column attached to a Vac-Man® Vacuum Manifold port. See Quick Start Guide for Assembly of a Vacuum Apparatus with the Welch Vacuum Pump Technical Bulletin #TB355 for setup details. Using the Reservoir Extension Funnel allows up to 100 mL of sample mixture to be added to the PureYield™ Binding Column at one time. Due to the size of the assembled binding column and funnel, we suggest processing 6 to 8 samples at a time per vacuum manifold.

steps.title: Pour the mixture from each tube...
steps.instruction: Pour the mixture from each tube from Step 8 into the Reservoir Extension Funnel on the PureYield™ Binding Column (combine both tubes of the same sample if applicable), turn on the pump and apply vacuum to capture TNA on the column. Periodically, empty the liquid waste collected in the blue Vac-Man® Laboratory Vacuum Manifold (Cat.# A7231). Dispose of the alcohol-containing waste following your institutional policies.

steps.title: Add 5 mL of Column Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL of Column Wash 1 (CWE) and apply a vacuum to pull the liquid through the PureYield™ Binding Column. Ensure that 57 mL of isopropanol has been added to the Column Wash 1 (CWE) bottle prior to use as described in the "Before Start" section.

steps.title: Add 20 mL of Column Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 20 mL of Column Wash 2 (RWA) and apply a vacuum to pull the liquid through the PureYield™ Binding Column. Continue to draw a vacuum for an additional 00:00:30 after all visible liquid has passed through the membrane. Ensure that 350 mL of 95 % (v/v) ethanol has been added to Column Wash 2 (RWA) bottle prior to use as described in the "Before Start" section.

steps.title: Release the vacuum and remove the...
steps.instruction: Release the vacuum and remove the column from the vacuum manifold. Preheat 1.2 mL of Nuclease-Free Water, per sample, to 60 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Assemble the elution device by placing a 1
steps.instruction: Assemble the elution device by placing a 1.5ml microcentrifuge tube into the base of the Eluator™ Vacuum Elution Device (Cat.# A1071) and securing the tube cap in the open position, as shown (Figure 2). Insert the PureYield™ Binding Column into the top of the Eluator Device, making sure the column is fully seated on the collar as shown in Figure 3. Figure 2. A 1.5ml microcentrifuge tube is placed in the base of the Eluator™ Vacuum Elution Device with the tube cap locked as shown. Figure 3. The final Eluator™ Vacuum Elution Device assembly, including the binding column, ready for use on a vacuum manifold.

steps.title: Place the Eluator™ Device assembly onto...
steps.instruction: Place the Eluator™ Device assembly onto a vacuum manifold (Figure 3). Add 500 µL of preheated ( 60 °C ) Nuclease-Free Water to the PureYield™ Binding Column. Apply maximum vacuum for 00:01:00 or until all liquid has passed through the column. Repeat the process by adding another 500 µL of preheated Nuclease-Free Water to the PureYield™ Binding Column to elute a total of 1 mL of TNA solution.

steps.title: Add 400 µL of Binding Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of Binding Buffer 1(BBD) and 100 µL of Binding Buffer 2 (BBE) to 1 mL of liquid eluted in Step 15.

steps.title: Mix well by inverting the tube...
steps.instruction: Mix well by inverting the tube gently 10 times, or until thoroughly mixed. Divide the contents into two DNA LoBind Tube 1.5 mL (Microcentrifuge tube, Eppendorf, 022431021, https://online-shop.eppendorf.us/US-en/Laboratory-Consumables-44512/Tubes-44515/DNA-LoBind-Tubes-PF-56252.html ) containing 750 µL each.

steps.title: 00:01:00 Add 750 µL of isopropanol...
steps.instruction: 00:01:00 Add 750 µL of isopropanol to each tube and mix well by inverting the tube gently 10 times, or until thoroughly mixed.

steps.title: Place the PureYield™ Minicolumn into a PureYield™ Collection Tube
steps.instruction: Place the PureYield™ Minicolumn into a PureYield™ Collection Tube. Pass the entire volume of the mixture through the column, 750 µL at a time (a total of four times), using a microcentrifuge at 10.000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute .

steps.title: Add 300 µL of Column Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL of Column Wash 1 (CWE) and pull through the PureYield™ Minicolumn by centrifugation 10000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute . Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Column Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Column Wash 2 (RWA) and pull through the PureYield™ Minicolumn by centrifugation 10000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute . Repeat this wash one time. Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 rpm, Room temperature, 30...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 rpm, Room temperature, 30 seconds to remove any residual wash solution.

steps.title: Preheat 65 µL of Nuclease-Free Water...
steps.instruction: Preheat 65 µL of Nuclease-Free Water per sample to 60 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Transfer the PureYield™ Minicolumn to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the PureYield™ Minicolumn to a new DNA LoBind tubes, 1.5 mL (Tubes, Eppendorf, 022431021, 1.5 mL, https://online-shop.eppendorf.us/US-en/Laboratory-Consumables-44512/Tubes-44515/DNA-LoBind-Tubes-PF-56252.html ) and add 30 µL of preheated ( 60 °C ) Nuclease-Free Water to the column. Let the water soak into the column filter for approximately 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge 10000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute to elute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute to elute. Repeat elution with another 30 µL of preheated Nuclease-Free Water, for a total of 60 µL .

steps.title: Store sample at or below -20 °C until further analysis
steps.instruction: Store sample at or below -20 °C until further analysis. TNA purified using this method can be directly used for RT-qPCR, for example as input into the following protocol: RTqPCR of SARS-CoV-2 N1 Target on ABI 7500 Fast Using Promega GoTaq Enviro Wastewater SARS-CoV-2 System V1 (protocols.io)
